metadata.title: The Biological Advantage of Pattern
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown
metadata.credit_line: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Hiring decisions are largely non-rational: a recognizer forms an impression in roughly ninety seconds by pattern-matching. A generic skills-based resume reads as a Commodity Candidate, valued around forty to sixty thousand dollars. A clear professional archetype reads as a Defined Contributor, moving the candidate into a higher band. What matters is not what you can do but which character you embody, because recognition rewards a legible pattern.

artifactLines: Hiring decisions are largely non-rational: a recognizer forms an impression in roughly ninety seconds by pattern-matching.
A generic skills-based resume reads as a Commodity Candidate, valued around forty to sixty thousand dollars.
A clear professional archetype reads as a Defined Contributor, moving the candidate into a higher band.
What matters is not what you can do but which character you embody, because recognition rewards a legible pattern.

beats.narration_text: The ninety-second, non-rational decision and the ladder from Commodity Candidate to Defined Contributor, where salary tracks which character you embody rather than what you can do, reframed how I read a resume. I want to engineer that recognition deliberately. Can you (1) explain the biological reason a recognizer defaults to pattern-matching a character in seconds, (2) rewrite a generic skills-based resume line into one that signals a clear professional archetype, and (3) show the specific cues that move a candidate from the 40-to-60 to the 60-to-80 thousand band?

output: You sit across from the HubSpot hiring manager

beats.narration_text: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — building real projects and shipping real research.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'The Biological Advantage of Pattern' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: Take the central idea from 'The Biological Advantage of Pattern' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then name the failure mode I'm most likely to hit.
